Container.Name: binhex-trimarr
Container.Overview: Removes (trims) unwanted audio and subtitles from matroska container format video files using mkvmerge. Features recursive scanning, smart skip via SQLite tracking, commentary track safety, multi-language support, auto-managed mkvmerge binary, and space savings reporting. Supports one-shot or cron-scheduled operation.
Container.Requires:
**Media directory** You must map at least one media directory (/media or custom path) containing your .mkv files.
**Language configuration** The TRIMARR_LANGUAGE variable must be set to one or more ISO 639-2 language codes (comma-separated), e.g. 'eng' for English only or 'eng,fre' for English and French.
**First run** On first run, trimarr downloads the mkvmerge binary automatically. Subsequent runs check for updates unless TRIMARR_NO_UPDATE_CHECK is set to 'yes'.
**Schedule (optional)** Set TRIMARR_SCHEDULE to a cron expression to run repeatedly (e.g. '0 2 * * *' for daily at 2am). Omit for one-shot mode (container exits after processing).
Container.DonateText: If you appreciate my work, then please consider buying me a beer :D

Config.Name: Path: /config
Config.Description: Container path for configuration files, database (trimarr.db), and logs.

Config.Name: Path: /media
Config.Description: Container path to your media files (e.g. movies, tv). Matches the default TRIMARR_MEDIA_PATH.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_LANGUAGE
Config.Description: (Required) ISO 639-2 language code(s) for tracks to keep. Comma-separate multiple codes, e.g. 'eng' or 'eng,fre'. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-2_codes

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_MEDIA_PATH
Config.Description: Directories containing media files to process. Use pipe (|) as separator for multiple paths, e.g. /media/Movies|/media/TV

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_SCHEDULE
Config.Description: Cron schedule for repeated runs, e.g. '0 2 * * *' (daily at 2am), '@daily', '*/30 * * * *'. Omit for one-shot mode (container exits after processing).

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_EDIT_METADATA_TITLE
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', each file's title metadata is updated to match its filename.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_DELETE_METADATA_TITLE
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', title metadata is deleted from files. Mutually exclusive with TRIMARR_EDIT_METADATA_TITLE.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_KEEP_SUBTITLES
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', all subtitle tracks are kept regardless of language.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_KEEP_AUDIO
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', all audio tracks are kept regardless of language.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_NO_BACKUP
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', original files are deleted instead of renamed to .bak after successful processing.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_DRY_RUN
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', performs a dry run without modifying any files. Defaults to 'yes' for safety — set to 'no' to allow actual processing.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_STRIP_LOWER_CHANNELS
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', audio tracks with channel count below the maximum surviving track are removed (e.g. drops 2ch if 8ch exists).

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_STRIP_COMMENTARY
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', audio and subtitle tracks whose name contains 'commentary' (case-insensitive) are removed.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_STRIP_SUBTITLE_REGEX
Config.Description: Python regex pattern. Any subtitle track whose name matches is removed after language filtering, regardless of language. Uses Python re syntax. Example: '(?i)songs.*signs'

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_RUN_ON_START
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', fires one run immediately on startup before the first scheduled cron fire. Requires TRIMARR_SCHEDULE to be set.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Logging level. Dropdown shows valid choices; INFO is default.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_DATABASE_PATH
Config.Description: Path to SQLite database for tracking processed files.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_LOG_PATH
Config.Description: Path to log file for tracking application events.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_MKVMERGE_PATH
Config.Description: Path to mkvmerge executable. When omitted, trimarr manages its own binary (auto-downloaded and updated).

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_PRE_PROCESS
Config.Description: Shell command to run before processing a directory. Use {leaf} for basename, {dir} for full path. Example: 'no_ransom.sh --unlock yes {leaf}'

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_POST_PROCESS
Config.Description: Shell command to run after processing a directory. Use {leaf} for basename, {dir} for full path. Example: 'no_ransom.sh --unlock no {leaf}'

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MINS
Config.Description: Timeout in minutes for each pre/post process command. Set to 0 to disable timeout.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_NO_UPDATE_CHECK
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', skips the automatic check for a newer mkvmerge version.

Config.Name: Variable: TRIMARR_SKIP_SIZE_CHECK
Config.Description: If set to 'yes', bypasses the 50% output size guard. Use when legitimate remuxes produce significantly smaller files.

Config.Name: Variable: ENABLE_STARTUP_SCRIPTS
Config.Description: Specify whether to run startup scripts to install and/or configure additional applications.

Config.Name: Variable: ENABLE_HEALTHCHECK
Config.Description: Enable or disable healthchecks.

Config.Name: Variable: HEALTHCHECK_COMMAND
Config.Description: The command or script to execute, if not specified then the script healthcheck.sh will be used (process, dns and https checking).

Config.Name: Variable: HEALTHCHECK_ACTION
Config.Description: The action to execute if the healthcheck command returns a non zero exit code, if not specified the action will be 'exit 1', if you want the container to exit on failure then set the action to 'kill 1'.

Config.Name: Variable: HEALTHCHECK_HOSTNAME
Config.Description: The hostname used when performing HTTPS and DNS healthchecking.

Config.Name: Variable: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for the running container

Config.Name: Variable: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for the running container

Config.Name: Variable: UMASK
Config.Description: UMASK for the running container
